meta.description: Evaluate diagnostic model performance with ROC curves, AUC, sensitivity, specificity, and optimal threshold selection. Inputs: predicted scores or probabilities, binary outcome labels. Outputs: ROC plot, AUC with 95% CI, confusion matrix, threshold table.

scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated scientific claims, invented numeric outputs, or unsupported conclusions were observed across the audit inputs.

practice_boundaries.detail: The skill stayed inside research-analysis scope and did not provide diagnosis or treatment advice.

methodological_ground.detail: The workflow uses an appropriate binary logistic-regression plus ROC design for case-control biomarker evaluation.

code_usability.detail: All valid runs were executable and invalid inputs failed cleanly with actionable SKILL_* errors.

functional_suitability.note: The skill covers the promised diagnostic logistic-regression and ROC workflow with aligned CLI, outputs, references, and retained expected outputs.

reliability.note: Input validation, overwrite protection, deterministic execution, reproducibility logging, and controlled boundary failures are all present.

performance_context.note: Progressive disclosure is well layered and the core workflow is concise for a moderate-complexity analysis skill.

agent_usability.note: Trigger boundaries, usage examples, argument tables, and retained smoke-test assets make the skill easy to apply from a cold start.

human_usability.note: Trigger language is natural, though the task remains specialized to case-control biomarker ROC analysis.

security.note: Inputs are validated, no unsafe code execution patterns are present, and local paths are logged intentionally for provenance.

maintainability.note: Responsibilities are cleanly split across CLI, validation, IO, core analysis, plotting, orchestration, and utilities, with every script under the 150-line project guideline and the data-preparation path further decomposed into smaller helpers.

agent_specific.note: Trigger precision, progressive disclosure, idempotent reruns, escape boundaries, and preserved expected outputs are all strong.

inputs.label: Default diagnostic run
inputs.note: Completed successfully and produced the documented data, table, plot, and metadata outputs.

assertions.text: The documented main CLI path is runnable as described.
assertions.note: Rscript scripts/main.R completed successfully.

assertions.text: The run emits the documented data, table, and plot artifacts.
assertions.note: analysis_data.rds, roc_model.rds, model_coefficients.csv, roc_auc_summary.csv, and roc_curve.pdf were present.

assertions.text: The workflow records reproducibility metadata.
assertions.note: session_info.txt captured parameters, package versions, and the seed.

assertions.text: The output stays within the stated diagnostic ROC scope.
assertions.note: The run produced diagnostic-model and ROC artifacts only, without calibration or survival outputs.

inputs.label: Explicit group column + custom ROC
inputs.note: Accepted an explicit grouping column and custom styling parameters while preserving the standard output bundle.

assertions.text: The skill accepts an explicit group column override.
assertions.note: --group_col group parsed successfully and was recorded in session_info.txt.

assertions.text: Valid custom visualization options do not break the workflow.
assertions.note: Custom width, height, title, and legend position were accepted and the ROC PDF was produced.

assertions.text: The output structure remains stable under non-default styling.
assertions.note: The same standard data and table outputs were still produced.

assertions.text: The workflow remains deterministic and auditable.
assertions.note: The seeded invocation completed and wrote reproducibility metadata.

inputs.label: Invalid case-group rejection
inputs.note: Correctly rejected an invalid case-group label before model fitting with a specific validation error.

assertions.text: The skill rejects an invalid case-group label before model fitting.
assertions.note: Execution stopped during dataset preparation.

assertions.text: The failure message is specific and actionable.
assertions.note: The error identifies the group column that was checked.

assertions.text: The failure does not fabricate analytical output.
assertions.note: No tables or plots were generated for the invalid run.

assertions.text: The boundary behavior matches the documented contract.
assertions.note: The rejection is consistent with the troubleshooting guide and error table.

inputs.label: Retained expected-output refresh
inputs.note: The documented smoke-test helper rebuilt tests/expected_output successfully and preserved the documented artifact set.

assertions.text: The R smoke-test helper is runnable as documented.
assertions.note: Rscript tests/run_smoke_test.R executed successfully.

assertions.text: The helper refreshes the retained expected-output bundle.
assertions.note: tests/expected_output contained the documented files after execution.

assertions.text: The helper preserves reproducibility metadata.
assertions.note: tests/expected_output/session_info.txt was regenerated and non-empty.

assertions.text: The helper stays within the skill's documented scope.
assertions.note: It simply wraps the main diagnostic ROC workflow using bundled test data.

inputs.label: Overwrite guard
inputs.note: The first seeded run succeeded with overwrite enabled and the second rerun was blocked cleanly by overwrite protection.

assertions.text: The skill prevents silent replacement of an existing result bundle.
assertions.note: The second invocation was blocked before any overwrite occurred.

assertions.text: The overwrite error message is specific and actionable.
assertions.note: It explicitly instructs the operator to use --overwrite.

assertions.text: The initial seeded run still produced a complete audit trail.
assertions.note: The first invocation created the expected bundle and session_info.txt.

assertions.text: The protection mechanism stays inside the skill's normal operational scope.
assertions.note: It adds safety without changing analysis semantics.

key_strengths: The skill now matches the expected data-analysis layout closely, including dedicated CLI, validation, IO, plotting, orchestration, references, test data, and retained expected outputs.
The analytical workflow is reproducible: seeded execution, parameter capture, and session_info.txt are present for both retained and audit outputs.
The standards refactor improved the operational contract: dependency checks are centralized, error codes follow the agreed naming set, and the data-preparation path is more maintainable.
The retained tests/expected_output bundle makes the skill easy to review, validate, and reuse in future audits.

recommendations.title: Add a small testthat suite
recommendations.problem: The skill includes a solid executable smoke test, but it still uses a custom assertion harness rather than a formal testthat suite.
recommendations.root_cause: The skill prioritizes a simple runnable integration test over a package-style testing framework.
recommendations.fix: Add one or two testthat files that call the smoke workflow in a temporary output directory and assert the expected artifact manifest and a controlled boundary failure.

recommendations.title: Document partial missing-marker continuation
recommendations.problem: The skill warns and continues when some requested marker genes are missing, but there is no explicit example of that behavior in the top-level docs.
recommendations.root_cause: The main documentation prioritizes the successful path and invalid hard-stop scenarios.
recommendations.fix: Add a short example to references/cli-guide.md showing that partially missing markers are skipped with a warning while valid markers continue to run.
